nodes.title: The Sketch Engine

deep_analysis.title: The Sketch Engine
deep_analysis.problem: The challenge of efficiently understanding and organizing a word's grammatical and collocational behavior for creating dictionary entries.
deep_analysis.method:
The Sketch Engine's 'word sketch' function provides a one-page summary of a word's grammatical and collocational behavior.

**Explanation:** The 'word sketch' function systematically analyzes a corpus to identify and organize recurring patterns for a word, such as collocations and idiomatic expressions. This allows lexicographers to have a draft dictionary entry that can be easily edited and published. It simplifies the lexicography process by providing a comprehensive, organized view of a word's usage without manual sorting through data.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The Sketch Engine's reliance on the Manatee database management system may not be ideal for handling text data where the sequence of words is crucial, as text sequence can be better managed by databases specifically designed for such tasks.
- The creation of compelling examples for dictionary entries using corpora remains a challenge due to the significant editorial time required, indicating limitations in automating the process of selecting 'good' examples.
- The recent collaboration for Arabic language support faces challenges intrinsic to the language itself, which may limit the Sketch Engine's effectiveness or require additional adaptations to handle Arabic text efficiently.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Explore integration with new and emerging devices to take advantage of advancements in connectivity and technology that have occurred over the past decade.
- Investigate ways to further expand and enhance multilingual capabilities, ensuring that the service continues to support a wide range of languages.
- Develop additional tools and features to aid in the creation and maintenance of user-generated corpora, allowing for more customization and user engagement with the software.
- Continue innovating and introducing new reports to keep up with the evolving needs of users and incorporate feedback to enhance user experience.

problem_evidence.text: The word sketch can be seen as a draft dictionary entry. The system has worked its way through the corpus to find all the recurring patterns for the word and has organised them, ready for the lexicographer to edit, elucidate, and publish.

method_evidence.text: The word sketch can be seen as a draft dictionary entry. The system has worked its way through the corpus to find all the recurring patterns for the word and has organised them, ready for the lexicographer to edit, elucidate, and publish.

limitation_evidence.text: The Sketch Engine prepares a 'distributional thesaurus' for a corpus. This is a thesaurus created on the basis of common collocation. If two words have many collocates in common, they will appear in each other's thesaurus entry. It works as follows: if we find instances of both drink tea and drink coffee, that is one small piece of evidence that tea and coffee are similar. We can say that they 'share' the collocate drink (verb), in the OBJECT-OF relation. In a very large computation, for all pairs of words, we compute how many collocates they share, and the ones that share most (after normalisation) are the ones that appear in a word's thesaurus entry. Distributional thesauruses are a topic of great interest in computational linguistics, and show promise for addressing a range of challenges.

A simple JSON API allows other programmes to access word sketches, collocations, thesaurus entries and to find the terminology in a document. 6.2 GDEX Dictionary users like examples. This is a clear finding of dictionary user research (Frankenberg Garcia 2014). Where the dictionary is to be published on paper, not many examples can be offered owing to space limitations. With electronic dictionaries, that constraint disappears. The constraint becomes, rather the editorial time needed to prepare them. There are already compelling linguistic reasons for taking examples from corpora rather than inventing them (Hanks 2012): could the corpus software not merely find the examples for a word, but automatically find the good ones, for using as dictionary examples?

This is all at the publishing end of the dictionary business. What about the lexicography end? Here, we have seen the corpus revolution (Hanks 2012). It started in Northern Europe in the 1980s and 1990s, and has been spreading. For Chinese, a first thoroughly corpus-based dictionary was probably Huang et al. (1997)'s classifier-noun collocation dictionary. For Arabic, it is Oxford University Press's Oxford Arabic Dictionary (Arts 2014), foot_0 though this was not produced in Asia. In Japan, corpus lexicography started in bilingual dictionary projects such as the WISDOM English-Japanese Dictionary (Sanseido 2003, 2007), but a truly corpus-based monolingual dictionary of Japanese is yet to appear.

However, for text, the sequence of words is the central fact, not the relations, so it is debatable whether SQL databases are suitable. The Sketch Engine is based on its own database management system called Manatee (Rychly ´2000, 2007) devised specifically for corpus linguistics. The web-based front end of Manatee is called Bonito and together with the Corpus Architect module (responsible for building and managing user corpora) these three are the core components of the Sketch Engine.

The collaboration for Arabic is more recent, with the Centre for Computational Linguistics at Columbia University, USA (who prepared MADA + TOKAN, the leading tools for tokenisation, lemmatisation and POS-tagging of Arabic) and Arabic experts elsewhere in the USA. in Saudi Arabia and in the UK. Over a number of years we had received many expressions of interest regarding Arabic in the Sketch Engine. But the language presents a number of challenges:

future_work_evidence.text: The Sketch Engine is a leading corpus tool (both in the sense of 'corpus query tool' and in the sense of 'corpus web service'). It is now 10 years old: a 10-year period that has seen revolutions in connectivity, devices, and dictionary publishing, and the worldwide spread of corpus methods in dictionary-making. It is mature software offering a wide range of functions, with the web service offering many corpora for many languages, as well as services for corpus building and maintenance.

In this paper we have described word sketches, concordancing, and the thesaurus (Sect. 2), the different kinds of user (Sect. 3), and approaches to working with many different languages (Sect. 4). Section 5 reviewed the kinds of corpora available in the Sketch Engine, including user corpora and the ways of building and working with them. Section 6 gives a brief tour of some of the innovations and new reports offered in the past few years. In Sect. 7 we reviewed related work.
As the strapline has it, 'corpora for all!'

nodes.rag_limitation:
- The Sketch Engine's reliance on the Manatee database management system may not be ideal for handling text data where the sequence of words is crucial, as text sequence can be better managed by databases specifically designed for such tasks.
- The creation of compelling examples for dictionary entries using corpora remains a challenge due to the significant editorial time required, indicating limitations in automating the process of selecting 'good' examples.
- The recent collaboration for Arabic language support faces challenges intrinsic to the language itself, which may limit the Sketch Engine's effectiveness or require additional adaptations to handle Arabic text efficiently.
nodes.rag_method:
The Sketch Engine's 'word sketch' function provides a one-page summary of a word's grammatical and collocational behavior.

**Explanation:** The 'word sketch' function systematically analyzes a corpus to identify and organize recurring patterns for a word, such as collocations and idiomatic expressions. This allows lexicographers to have a draft dictionary entry that can be easily edited and published. It simplifies the lexicography process by providing a comprehensive, organized view of a word's usage without manual sorting through data.
nodes.rag_problem: The challenge of efficiently understanding and organizing a word's grammatical and collocational behavior for creating dictionary entries.
nodes.rag_future_work:
- Explore integration with new and emerging devices to take advantage of advancements in connectivity and technology that have occurred over the past decade.
- Investigate ways to further expand and enhance multilingual capabilities, ensuring that the service continues to support a wide range of languages.
- Develop additional tools and features to aid in the creation and maintenance of user-generated corpora, allowing for more customization and user engagement with the software.
- Continue innovating and introducing new reports to keep up with the evolving needs of users and incorporate feedback to enhance user experience.

nodes.title: CORPUS LINGUISTICS: METHOD, THEORY, AND PRACTICE

deep_analysis.title: CORPUS LINGUISTICS: METHOD, THEORY, AND PRACTICE
deep_analysis.problem: Traditional linguistic analysis methods faced difficulties in processing large datasets to analyze language usage patterns due to technological limitations.
deep_analysis.method:
Development of computer-based corpus linguistics methodologies and tools, including concordance software, to automate and facilitate the analysis of large text corpora.

**Explanation:** Corpus linguistics utilizes computational tools to automate linguistic data analysis, allowing researchers to efficiently process large volumes of text data. Concordance software analyzes text to uncover patterns in word usage, context, and collocation, which aids in understanding the nuances of language more accurately compared to manual methods.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The method discussed in the paper still faces infrastructural challenges as earlier corpus processing required large computers at specific locations, which limits accessibility and flexibility.
- The current corpus methods cannot identify marked words, indicating a limitation in handling complex annotation systems in linguistic data processing.
- Developing corpus databases involves legal issues, such as copyright restrictions, which challenge the comprehensive data collection and distribution essential for corpus-based studies.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- 探索如何将计算机技术与语言学结合，以支持更广泛的跨学科研究。通过整合信息技术，可以提高语言学研究的深度和广度。
- 进一步拓展和应用语料库语言学在印尼语语料库建设中的应用，以便更全面地研究和理解该语言的特性和使用。
- 研究心理语言学与语料库语言学的综合应用，特别是在考试中的语言自然性评估和第二语言习得中的作用。

problem_evidence.text: Buku ini terdiri atas sembilan bab, yang diawali dengan bab kesatu, What is Corpus Linguistics... Alat berupa software komputer yang digunakan untuk melakukan konkordansi berkembang dari generasi ke generasi.

method_evidence.text: Buku ini terdiri atas sembilan bab, yang diawali dengan bab kesatu, What is Corpus Linguistics... Alat berupa software komputer yang digunakan untuk melakukan konkordansi berkembang dari generasi ke generasi.

limitation_evidence.text: Pada bab ketujuh, penulis memaparkan klasifikasi linguistik teoretis menjadi dua, yaitu fungsionalisme dan metodologis. Fungsionalisme menginvestigasi bentuk bahasa, tetapi menerangkannya menurut fungsi bahasa itu digunakan. Bahasa tidak dianggap sebagai sistem yang terisolasi namun sesuatu yang digunakan u n t u k m e n y a m p a i k a n m a k n a . Kelompok fungsionalisme selalu mempertimbangkan konteks dalam meneliti objek kajiannya.

Pada bab kedelapan McEnery dan Hardie (2003) menjelaskan hubungan antara linguistik korpus d an p s i k o l i n g u i s t i k d a n l i n g u i s t i k fungsionalis. Hubungan korpus dengan psikolinguistik adalah data korpus dapat digunakan untuk menguji kealamiahan bahasa yang digunakan dalam soal ujian (tes), juga dalam penguasaan bahasa kedua. McEnery dan Hardie (2003) juga menjelaskan perbedaan antara linguistik korpus dan linguistik komputasional (computational linguistics). Mereka mengatakan linguistik korpus bertujuan untuk mencari sifat dan penggunaan bahasa, sedangkan linguistik komputasional bertujuan memodelkan sifat bahasa dengan menggunakan komputer. Selain itu, memfokuskan dirinya pada pemecahan masalah teknis yang melibatkan bahasa.

Secara umum kehadiran buku ini bisa menginspirasi penelitian interdisipliner antara ilmu kebahasaan dengan menggunakan teknologi komputer. Kemajuan teknologi informasi dan komputer dapat bersinergi dengan ilmu kebahasaan atau linguistik sehingga dapat membantu penelitian linguistik lebih ekstensif. Perkembangan linguistik korpus juga dapat diterapkan lebih luas dalam membangun korpus bahasa Indonesia.

Penulis : Tony McEnery dan Andrew Hardie Tebal Buku : 294 halaman Edisi/ISBN : 2012/ 978-0-521-83851-1 Penerbit : Cambridge Buku ini merupakan versi pertama yang memaparkan bagaimana linguistik korpus berkembang dan digunakan sebagai metodologi, teori utama, dan penggunaan korpus dalam bidang linguistik dan lintas disiplin. Buku ini terdiri atas sembilan bab, yang diawali dengan bab kesatu, What is Corpus Linguistics? Penulis mendefinisikan linguistik korpus sebagai sebuah bidang yang memfokuskan pada prosedur, atau metode mempelajari, atau meneliti bahasa. McEnery dan Hardie juga menyinggung mengenai pendekatan yang digunakan dalam linguistik korpus yang juga dikemukakan oleh Tognini-Bonelli (2001). Tognini menyatakan adanya dua pendekatan linguistik korpus, yaitu berbasis korpus (corpus-based) dan yang dikendalikan korpus (corpusdriven). Keduanya memiliki perbedaan dalam melihat korpus sebagai bukti yang mendukung teori. Yang pertama menggunakan pendekatan yang bersifat deduktif. Sementara itu corpus-driven approach menganggap korpus sebagai bukti yang harus menjadi acuan teori, sehingga bersifat induktif. Bab kedua buku ini membahas cara menilai dan menganalisis data korpus. Cara menilai dan menganalisis data korpus dapat dimulai dari metadata, penanda teks (textual markup), dan anotasi. Metadata memberi informasi siapa yang memproduksi teks tersebut, kapan, dan dalam bahasa apa teks tersebut dibuat. Penanda teks meliputi tanda yang digunakan dalam penulisan teks itu sendiri seperti tipologi, misalnya cetak miring. Anotasi memberikan informasi mengenai fitur linguistik data tersebut, bisa berupa kelas kata seperti verba, nomina, ajektiva, atau adverbial. Hal lain yang paling penting dalam korpus adalah tersedianya alat yang memungkinkan untuk mencari sebuah korpus dalam rangkaian unit tertentu seperti kata atau frasa yang ditampilkan dalam format baris secara vertikal, atau disebut konkordansi. Alat berupa software komputer yang digunakan untuk melakukan konkordansi berkembang dari generasi ke generasi. Yang pertama menggunakan komputer besar yang ditempatkan di satu lokasi dan hanya menampilkan KWIC, serta perlu software lain untuk mengolah yang lainnya. Generasi kedua menggunakan Personal Computer (PC) sehingga setiap orang dapat menggunakannya dan lebih banyak lagi data yang dapat dimasukkan, program bukan hanya menggunakan konkordansi KWIC, tetapi juga bisa mengurut secara alfabetis konteks sebelah kiri dan kanan kata yang dicari, serta dapat melakukan perhitungan statistik deskriptif dasar, hanya tidak bisa mengidentifikasi kata yang ditandai. Generasi ketiga dapat mengolah data lebih besar melalui PC, sedangkan generasi keempat berbasis web. McEnery dan Hardie (2003) juga membahas penggunaan statistik dalam linguistik korpus. Perhitungan statistik yang dapat digunakan untuk mengolah data linguistik korpus meliputi statistik deskriptif dan tes signifikansi untuk menguji seberapa mungkin hasil tertentu merupakan sebuah kebetulan, misalnya dalam menghitung kata kunci dan kolokasi. Bab ketiga buku ini membahas permasalahan yang dihadapi dalam membuat korpus yang salah satunya adalah masalah hukum, khususnya hak cipta (copyright). Untuk memasukan dokumen sebagai data korpus perlu izin untuk dapat menampilkannya pada mesin pengolah korpus, apalagi bila disajikan pada web. Untuk mengantisipasi hal itu ada beberapa cara, pertama menghubungi pemegang hak cipta tersebut untuk meminta izin menggunakan dokumen untuk mesin pengolah korpus. Kedua adalah mengumpulkan data tanpa harus meminta izin, dengan konsekuensi hasilnya tidak didistribusikan kepada yang lain. Pada bab keempat, penulis memaparkan "Linguistik Korpus Bahasa Inggris" yang sudah dirintis oleh beberapa universitas, antara lain University College London, yang membuat korpus terbesar untuk studi komparatif variasi bahasa Inggris yaitu International Corpus of English (ICE). Lancaster University, dipelopori oleh Leech dan Garside (1971), membangun CLAWS (Constituent Likelihood Automatic Tagging System) yaitu sistem anotasi kelas kata yang otomatis. Di University of Birmingham dipelopori oleh Sinclair membuat corpus-based dictionary (Collins COBUILD dictionary). Universite Catholique de Louvain oleh Sylviane Granger membuat International Corpus of Lerner English (ICLE). University of Nothingham memelopori korpus bentuk lisan tata bahasa lisan dalam bahasa Inggris. Pada Northern Arizona University dan Amerika pengembangan korpus dipelopori oleh Biber yang membuat korpus seperti ARCHER (A Representative Corpus of Historical English Registers), AAACL (American Association of Applied Corpus Linguistics). Selain itu, LDC (Linguistics Data Consortium), MICASE (Michigan Corpus of Academic Spoken English), BYU (Brigham Young) dan merupakan pusat linguistik korpus di Amerika. Pada bab kelima, penulis memaparkan penelitian berbasis korpus variasi sinkronik dan diakronik. Banyak sekali karya berbasis korpus mengenai variasi register, antarwaktu, dan variabel internal lain. Sebagian karya variasi diakronik telah dilakukan dengan menggunakan metodologi umum perbandingan korpus antarwaktu (diakronik). Sebaliknya, penelitian variasi sinkronik berbeda bergantung pada variabel sosial atau registernya. Akan tetapi, keduanya dapat menggunakan metode kuantitatif ataupun kualitatif. Bab keenam buku ini menjabarkan Linguistik Korpus Neo Firthian yang terdiri dari kolokasi dan wacana (discourse). Kolokasi menunjukkan bahwa aspek penting makna sebuah kata bukan hanya terkandung didalamnya tetapi juga bergantung pada kata lain yang menyertainya. Caranya yaitu dengan meneliti pola yang muncul bersamaan dalam korpus. Wacana (discourse) mengacu pada unit bahasa di atas tingkatan kalimat. Analisis Wanaca Kritis (AWK) memberi makna lebih mengenai discourse bahwa discourse merupakan praktik, sebuah karakteristik bahasa yang ditemukan dalam sebuah kelompok teks atau lebih luas lagi dalam bahasa komunitas. Dalam bab ini penulis juga menyatakan bahwa prosodi semantik yang disebut oleh Stubb merupakan sebuah konsep yang berasal dari konkordansi berdasarkan analisis neo-Firthian tentang kolokasi. Hal lain yang menjadi perhatian neo-Fithtian adalah leksis dan tata bahasa yang meliputi prinsip idiom, pola tata bahasa, dan priming leksikal. Dikatakan pula bahwa korpus masih jadi perdebatan apakah dianggap data atau dapat dianggap teori.

Fungsionalisme menginvestigasi bentuk bahasa, tetapi menerangkannya menurut fungsi bahasa itu digunakan. Bahasa tidak dianggap sebagai sistem yang terisolasi namun sesuatu yang digunakan u n t u k m e n y a m p a i k a n m a k n a . Kelompok fungsionalisme selalu mempertimbangkan konteks dalam meneliti objek kajiannya.

Pada bab kedelapan McEnery dan Hardie (2003) menjelaskan hubungan antara linguistik korpus d an p s i k o l i n g u i s t i k d a n l i n g u i s t i k fungsionalis. McEnery dan Hardie (2003) juga menjelaskan perbedaan antara linguistik korpus dan linguistik komputasional (computational linguistics). Mereka mengatakan linguistik korpus bertujuan untuk mencari sifat dan penggunaan bahasa, sedangkan linguistik komputasional bertujuan memodelkan sifat bahasa dengan menggunakan komputer. Selain itu, memfokuskan dirinya pada pemecahan masalah teknis yang melibatkan bahasa.

nodes.title: Prose Comprehension beyond the Word

deep_analysis.title: Prose Comprehension beyond the Word
deep_analysis.problem: The difficulty of studying prose comprehension arises because it is guided by multiple domains of knowledge and contains critical cues between the lines that are not overtly expressed.
deep_analysis.method:
Develop a cognitive model that accounts for the implicit and explicit knowledge domains utilized in prose comprehension, emphasizing the interpretation of cues that reside 'between the lines'.

**Explanation:** Prose comprehension involves processing information from various knowledge domains, including cultural, linguistic, and contextual cues that are subtly embedded in text rather than overtly stated. By creating a model that integrates these components, researchers can better understand how individuals derive meaning from prose, even when explicit explanations are not provided. This allows for a more comprehensive analysis of the cognitive mechanisms involved.

nodes.title: Web-based interfaces for natural language processing tools

deep_analysis.title: Web-based interfaces for natural language processing tools
deep_analysis.problem: Students without a computer science background struggle to understand and interact with complex Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems.
deep_analysis.method:
Develop web-based interfaces for NLP technologies that allow experimentation and modification of knowledge bases.

**Explanation:** By providing web interfaces, students can interact with NLP systems using a user-friendly platform. These interfaces simplify the interaction, enabling students to experiment with different inputs and observe outputs and internal processes. Additionally, they allow modification of system knowledge bases, reflecting changes in behavior, facilitating learning through experimentation without needing deep technical expertise.
deep_analysis.limitation: 未找到明确的局限性描述
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Improve the scalability of web-based interfaces to accommodate a larger number of users and more complex Natural Language Processing models.
- Enhance user interface features to provide more intuitive guidance for students with diverse educational backgrounds, particularly those without a computer science background.
- Integrate additional customization options that allow students to experiment with modifying the models' parameters and observe the effects on output dynamically.
- Expand the range of supported Natural Language Processing tools to cover emerging technologies and new linguistic models for broader educational coverage.

nodes.title: Language and Computers

deep_analysis.title: Language and Computers
deep_analysis.problem: Current computational systems lack meaningful contextual understanding when generating creative text, such as poetry, leading to outputs that fail to be meaningful or relatable.
deep_analysis.method:
The integration of flowcharts and process nodes (FloWr) to guide the generative process and iterative text composition, enabling context-aware poetic and narrative generation.

**Explanation:** FloWr's flowchart system serves as a structure for understanding context and guiding text generation. By using flowcharts, computers can simulate a narrative framework, allowing systems to generate text that flows coherently and incorporates context and thematic elements. This process orientation helps bridge the gap between mere grammatical correctness and the creation of text that resonates emotionally and cognitively with human audiences, addressing the contextual deficiency in current computational poetry generation.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- Our method lacks a comprehensive multi-modal ability to associate words with various sensory and emotional contexts, similar to the human experience, which includes visual, auditory, olfactory, and emotional associations.
- Flowcharts used in our approach, while partially resembling neural processes, still require a training phase to effectively understand and express language, indicating a need for further development to achieve more advanced language capabilities.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Develop a TLC-like library using FloWr for creating "form tests" and generative tools that incorporate a multi-modal aspect into knowledge representation, enhancing understanding of linguistic meaning beyond traditional methods.
- Conduct experiments with natural language that aim to express nuances and emotional aspects of language, moving beyond conventional parsing and text generation approaches to capture the "feel" of language.
- Investigate computational processes that model text generation in contextual, procedural, and gestural ways to gain deeper insights into how linguistic meanings are formed.
- Explore methodologies for motivating language learners to express themselves confidently, as a strategy to advance understanding and practical application of language acquisition theories.

problem_evidence.text: FloWr's ProcessNodes define a micro-language denoting the available ways in which the system can transform input data to output data.

method_evidence.text: FloWr's ProcessNodes define a micro-language denoting the available ways in which the system can transform input data to output data.

limitation_evidence.text: Language is also very multi-modal, and if we think again in terms of neurons and brain pathways, the word "dog" not only brings up images of a furry canine animal but all kinds of other associations. Pet dogs. Large and small dogs. Dogs with different color fur (visual cortex). Dog as a verb (motor cortex associations). The smell of wet dogs (olfactory bulb). Fear of dogs (limbic system). Favorite pet dogs you have loved in your life (emotions). The spelling or sound of the word D-O-G as opposed to D-A-W-G (auditory and visual cortex). If you are from certain countries perhaps even dog meat. Imagining a "demon dog" will invoke networks running all over the brain [Schlegel et al., 2013] . Now what about computers, do they have a similar symbolic or multi-modal operating ability? People, as they become literate, learn to write and sound out letters at the same time -and the foundations of first language learning draw heavily on a sensory-motor channel [Iverson and Thelen, 1999; Hernandez, 2013] .

Computers may be said to have a first language with several dialects, highly constrained by grammar -namely, bytecode and programming languages. The flowcharts described above begin to recover a degree of multi-modality, and a process orientation that is similar in certain partial respects to sensory-motor experience. We might also think of flowcharts as akin to neurons and cortexes, as above. After all, there is a kind of embodiment even in the brain -it is an active and evolving organ [Doidge, 2007] . Flowcharts are rather different from classical neural network models, but one common feature is that they would need to be "trained" if they are to understand and express language.

Again, poetry could be part of the way forward. Exercises from a book like Writing Simple Poems: Pattern Poetry for Language Acquisition [Holmes and Moulton, 2001] might be used to teach computers as well as humans. Note that grammar and poetry are very different, and perhaps complementary. Thus, for example, a "Learn English!" notebook found in Japan with Subject -Verb -Direct Object -Indirect Object -Prepositional Phrase -Adjectival Phrase written at the top of each page offers a useful rubric for Japanese students, since the verb comes at the end of the sentence in Japanese (SOV). But grammar will only get you so far. Consider Chomsky's famous nonsense statement, "Colorless green ideas sleep furiously." A poetic gesture -like prefixing that phrase with a description of "planted tulip bulbs" -is able to make some sense out of nonsense by adding context! 3 Social context is also likely to be relevant: peer learning is very useful for human language learners [Rancière, 1991; Raw, 2014] . One idea would be to adapt the Q&A model mentioned in Section 2 as a "social" site about poetry.

In this section, we will briefly review human language learning from a second language teaching perspective, and then draw comparisons with the foregoing description of a hypothetical computer language learner.

First, why are primary language learning and second/ foreign language learning often considered separately? One of the biggest differences between the two cases is that after learning a first language, "neural pathways" have been set down, so that second language information has to be encoded "along with" or "beside" the first language pathways. New neural connections have to be formed to maintain the memory of the second language, whereas the first language has been implanted quite thoroughly. So when learning verbs, nouns, etc., the person (child, adult) learns or has learned, for instance, "stand up!" first and then that "levantate!" means a similar thing, in Spanish, or that "chair" = "silla." As they progress in the second language, what we find is a process that linguists call "interlanguage" [Selinker, 1972] , or a language that is not quite English, and not totally Spanish yet, because of the variations in the two languages in structure, phonemes, morphemes, allophones, semantics, and so on.

future_work_evidence.text: Quillian's "The Teachable Language Comprehender: A Simulation Program and Theory of Language" [1969] took the novel -and fundamental -approach of understanding things in such a way that new understandings could be added directly to its knowledge base. 4 When reading a piece of text, the TLC program would search its memory for related information that it could use to make sense of the input. More specifically, a given text would be expanded using "form tests" which extracted meaningful pieces of the text, and connected these to items stored in memory. Quillian writes that "ultimately, a human-like memory should relate descriptive knowledge of the world to perceptual-motor activity, in a manner like that indicated by Piaget" -but deems this to be "far beyond our present scope" [Quillian, 1969, p. 474] .

Future research might use FloWr to develop a TLC-like library of "form tests" and generative tools that would add a multi-modal aspect to knowledge representation. To be sure, a flowchart-based representation of poetic process would be quite different from the embodied sensory-motor experience of humans. Nevertheless, computational processes that allow us to model text generation contextually, procedurally, and gesturally can help to understand the way linguistic meaning comes to be. This is not something we can readily learn from parsing, corpus-based modeling, or grammar-based text generation alone. There is exciting potential for future experiments with natural language that strives to capture and express shades of meaning and the "feel" of the language. Experimentation is necessary: if we have learned anything about language, it is that "learners should be motivated to speak bravely" [Wang, 2014] .

problem_evidence.text: L’uomo è l’animale parlante... Ma siamo sicuri di conoscerli a fondo?

method_evidence.text: L’uomo è l’animale parlante... Ma siamo sicuri di conoscerli a fondo?

future_work_evidence.text: Tutto ciò che hai sempre voluto sapere sul linguaggio e sulle lingue

L’uomo è l’animale parlante: la parola è, infatti, ciò che più ci rende umani, ciò che ci distingue come specie rispetto agli altri animali. La lingua e il linguaggio sono i nostri inseparabili compagni di viaggio, li usiamo ogni giorno, un numero incalcolabile di volte. Ma siamo sicuri di conoscerli a fondo?\nOgni giorno usiamo l’automobile senza necessariamente capire tutti i meccanismi che la fanno funzionare. Lo stesso accade con lingua e linguaggio: li usiamo in maniera automatica...

nodes.title: Teaching NLP/CL through games

deep_analysis.title: Teaching NLP/CL through games
deep_analysis.problem: Students lack the necessary skills to conduct computational experiments in NLP/CL courses.
deep_analysis.method:
Introduce games designed to teach NLP/CL concepts, specifically the parsing process, without requiring advanced computational skills.

**Explanation:** The use of games provides an interactive, skill-independent method to teach complex concepts like parsing by simplifying them into terms that can be understood and explored even by those without strong computational backgrounds. Games make the learning process engaging and can be tailored to align with teaching goals, thus bridging the gap created by students' skill deficiencies.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The approach may be limited by the students' varying levels of engagement and adaptability to game-based learning, which might not be suitable for all educational contexts or learning styles.
- Our method may not fully replace computational experiments since games can simplify complex NLP/CL processes, potentially limiting students' understanding of advanced topics that require technical experiments.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Develop new game formats specifically tailored to other areas of NLP/CL beyond parsing, to enhance the teaching scope and address varied topics within the field.
- Investigate how adapting game rules can optimize learning experiences for different student skill levels in computational experiments, aiming to improve usability and effectiveness.
- Explore the integration of digital platforms for these educational games to increase accessibility and engagement among students.

nodes.title: Expanding horizons in the study of World Englishes with the 1.9 billion word Global Web-based English Corpus (GloWbE)

deep_analysis.title: Expanding horizons in the study of World Englishes with the 1.9 billion word Global Web-based English Corpus (GloWbE)
deep_analysis.problem: There is a lack of large-scale, diverse data sources to study variations in World Englishes across different dialects and countries.
deep_analysis.method:
The creation of the GloWbE Corpus, a 1.9 billion word Global Web-based English Corpus gathered from 20 different English-speaking countries.

**Explanation:** The GloWbE Corpus provides a vast and diverse dataset that encompasses both informal and formal genres from a wide range of web pages, enabling researchers to examine linguistic variations in World Englishes across different countries and dialects. Its large size and structured interface facilitate the study of dialectal differences that were previously difficult to analyze due to limitations in existing datasets.
deep_analysis.limitation: 未找到明确的局限性描述
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Explore dialect variations by conducting cross-country comparative studies using the GloWbE corpus to identify unique linguistic patterns across the 20 English-speaking countries represented in the dataset.
- Investigate the impact of informal digital communication on language change by analyzing the 60 percent of the corpus derived from informal blogs, providing insights into contemporary language usage trends.
- Develop methodologies to improve the accuracy and depth of linguistic analysis within large-scale web-based corpora like GloWbE, potentially addressing challenges posed by the diverse genres and text types included.

nodes.title: The Effects of Corpus Use on Second Language Vocabulary Learning: A Multilevel Meta-analysis

deep_analysis.title: The Effects of Corpus Use on Second Language Vocabulary Learning: A Multilevel Meta-analysis
deep_analysis.problem: Traditional second language vocabulary learning methods may not effectively leverage contextual usage and authentic language exposure, leading to less effective vocabulary retention and comprehension.
deep_analysis.method:
Utilizing corpus-based learning techniques, which provide learners with access to real language usage and contextual examples, enhancing vocabulary learning and retention.

**Explanation:** Corpus-based learning allows students to encounter words in their natural context, thereby improving understanding and retention. By using a corpus, learners can see how words are used in different contexts, which aids in learning nuanced meanings and improves long-term retention. The meta-analysis shows a medium-sized positive effect on vocabulary learning, indicating that corpus use helps learners effectively acquire and retain vocabulary.

nodes.title: ANNIS3: A new architecture for generic corpus query and visualization

deep_analysis.title: ANNIS3: A new architecture for generic corpus query and visualization
deep_analysis.problem: Richly annotated linguistic corpora often contain heterogeneous data with complex, potentially conflicting segmentation layers that are difficult to represent and visualize with generic corpus query tools.
deep_analysis.method:
ANNIS3 introduces a concept of multiple segmentation layers integrated into a general graph-based data model to manage conflicting annotations and provide versatile visualization capabilities.

**Explanation:** By utilizing a graph-based data model, ANNIS3 allows for the flexible representation of heterogeneous data and manages conflicts between different segmentation layers. This model supports generic querying and visualization, enabling users to efficiently interact with richly annotated corpora despite their complexity.
deep_analysis.limitation: 未找到明确的局限性描述
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Implement additional visualization methods to enhance the representation of heterogeneously annotated corpora and provide better insights into complex linguistic data.
- Explore the integration of more advanced conflict resolution mechanisms within the segmentation layers to effectively manage multiple, potentially conflicting data sources.
- Investigate the scalability of the system when dealing with large corpora and diverse annotation formats, ensuring performance remains robust as data complexity increases.

nodes.title: The Oxford English Dictionary

deep_analysis.title: The Oxford English Dictionary
deep_analysis.problem: 未找到明确的研究问题描述
deep_analysis.method: 未找到明确的方法描述
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The method used for identifying homonymy is conservative, potentially leading to errors by labeling homonymous senses as polysemous, rather than the other way around.
- The annotation layer produced is synthetic and while it achieved a high F1-score on a small evaluation set, the limited scope of the evaluation may not fully represent the method's efficacy on a broader scale.
- The current approach does not utilize phonetic information for homophony, which could improve the quality of identifying unrelated meanings that share the same phonetic form.
- The method lacks a complex model to create a high-quality sense-to-sense mapping to the Oxford English Dictionary (OED), which limits the ability to incorporate diachronic information useful for studying language change.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Enhance WordNet with phonetic information to infer homophony, expanding the lexical database with sound-based distinctions that are not evident through orthography alone.
- Develop more complex models to enable high-quality sense-to-sense mappings to the OED, allowing WordNet to incorporate diachronic information such as dates of sense emergence, which would aid in studying language change.

limitation_evidence.text: We present a new annotation layer for the Princeton WordNet, which splits senses into lemmas, making it possible to distinguish between polysemy and homonymy. We use a method which is conservative with respect to homonymy identification (we would rather erroneously label two homonymous senses as polysemous than vice versa, §3.1). Additionally, in contrast to previous work, we use an alignment-based method which will be able to correctly treat figurative polysemy. We create this annotation layer using a simple method that exploits recent advances in language modelling; although the annotation layer we produce is synthetic, the F1-score that our model attained on a small evaluation set that we produced was .97, indicating that it is of high quality.

In future work, we hope to enhance WordNet with more information. Lemmas in the OED are annotated with phonetic information; this could be used to infer homophony, which occurs which two unrelated meanings use the same phonetic form (even if they do not necessarily use the same orthographic form). An example is the word base, which is homophonous with the word bass. Additionally, if more complex models could be developed to produce a high quality senseto-sense mapping to the OED, then we could leverage information the fine-grained senses in the OED contain about the dates of sense emergence, to make WordNet diachronic. This would be very useful in the study of language change.

nodes.title: AntConc: A Learner and Classroom Friendly, Multi-Platform Corpus Analysis Toolkit

deep_analysis.title: AntConc: A Learner and Classroom Friendly, Multi-Platform Corpus Analysis Toolkit
deep_analysis.problem: Teachers and students need a user-friendly, multi-functional toolkit for corpus analysis that can be used across different platforms in educational settings.
deep_analysis.method:
AntConc provides a freeware, multi-platform corpus analysis toolkit specifically designed for classroom use, including tools like a concordancer, frequency generators, clustering and lexical bundle analysis tools, and a word distribution plot.

**Explanation:** By providing a comprehensive set of features in one toolkit, AntConc enables educators and students to perform detailed text analysis without needing multiple software tools. Its design caters specifically to classroom environments, ensuring that it's accessible to learners who may not have advanced technical skills, and its multi-platform availability ensures that it can be used regardless of the operating system, thereby addressing the accessibility and usability issues in educational contexts.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- AntConc currently has limitations in its software capabilities, which may affect its utility in certain classroom settings.
- The toolkit might not fully meet the needs of advanced corpus analysis tasks due to its freeware nature and design focused on educational environments.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Enhance the software to overcome current limitations, such as increasing compatibility or improving user interface, to make it more accessible and efficient for classroom usage.
- Expand the toolkit to include additional tools or features that cater to more advanced corpus analysis tasks, providing greater functionality for both novice and experienced users.
- Conduct user feedback studies to gather insights on user experience, focusing on identifying new features or improvements that users require or desire.

deep_analysis.title: DeepDict — A Graphical Corpus-based Dictionary of Word Relations
deep_analysis.problem: Traditional dictionaries are limited in showing real-life word relationships and usage patterns, often lacking dynamic insights from linguistic contexts.
deep_analysis.method:
DeepDict creates a graphical lexicon using grammatically analyzed corpus data to highlight co-occurrence strength between dependency pairs, providing detailed entries on complementation patterns and collocations.

**Explanation:** By using corpus data to analyze grammatical dependencies and co-occurrence strengths, DeepDict can automatically identify typical word relations that occur naturally, providing users with a richer and more contextually relevant understanding of word usage. The graphical interface further aids users in exploring these dynamic relationships, adjusting for various lexical frequencies.

deep_analysis.problem: A lack of large and diverse corpus of American English available for systematic linguistic analysis.
deep_analysis.method:
The creation of the Corpus of Contemporary American English (COCA), consisting of over 385 million words spanning from 1990 to 2008, balanced across different genres including spoken, fiction, popular magazines, newspapers, and academic journals.

**Explanation:** The COCA provides a comprehensive and balanced dataset that represents a wide range of contemporary American English usage across different contexts. This supports detailed linguistic analysis and research, filling a gap in available resources for studying American English systematically.

deep_analysis.problem: Determining conditions under which certain integrable operators can be expressed as squares of Hankel operators, which simplifies the analysis of these operators.
deep_analysis.method:
Identify sufficient conditions for an operator with a kernel of the form A(x)B(y) - A(y)B(x)/(x - y) to be expressed as the square of a Hankel operator.

**Explanation:** By representing these special class operators (Tracy-Widom type) as the square of Hankel operators, the mathematical properties of Hankel operators (such as trace and spectrum calculations) can be utilized to derive properties of the original operator. This allows for easier derivation of characteristics like trace formulas and spectral information.

nodes.title: Situation models in language comprehension and memory.

deep_analysis.title: Situation models in language comprehension and memory.
deep_analysis.problem: Understanding how mental representations, specifically situation models, are formed and utilized during language comprehension and memory retrieval.
deep_analysis.method:
The research focuses on establishing the existence and functionality of situation models, using empirical tasks designed to assess various dimensions of these models.

**Explanation:** Situation models are integrated mental representations that capture the described state of affairs during comprehension. By demonstrating the existence of these models through specific tasks, the research helps elucidate the mechanisms of language comprehension and memory retrieval, showing how individuals mentally simulate situations to process and remember information. This establishes a theoretical framework for identifying how language is understood and retained, emphasizing the role of mental simulation and context representation.

nodes.title: Constructing inferences during narrative text comprehension.

deep_analysis.title: Constructing inferences during narrative text comprehension.
deep_analysis.problem: During narrative text comprehension, readers need to construct knowledge-based inferences to fully understand and integrate information, but it is often unclear which inferences are necessary and how they are generated.
deep_analysis.method:
The constructionist theory posits that readers generate a referential situation model to construct the necessary inferences based on both the text and their prior knowledge, focusing on the construction of a selective subset of information under typical comprehension conditions.

**Explanation:** By utilizing a referential situation model, the constructionist theory helps readers selectively generate relevant inferences which are necessary for understanding narrative texts. This model integrates textual information with the reader's prior knowledge, thereby focusing on the most pertinent inferences needed for comprehension rather than overwhelming the reader with all possible information. This targeted approach simplifies the inferencing process and aids in the efficient comprehension of narrative texts.
deep_analysis.limitation:

nodes.rag_method:
The constructionist theory posits that readers generate a referential situation model to construct the necessary inferences based on both the text and their prior knowledge, focusing on the construction of a selective subset of information under typical comprehension conditions.

**Explanation:** By utilizing a referential situation model, the constructionist theory helps readers selectively generate relevant inferences which are necessary for understanding narrative texts. This model integrates textual information with the reader's prior knowledge, thereby focusing on the most pertinent inferences needed for comprehension rather than overwhelming the reader with all possible information. This targeted approach simplifies the inferencing process and aids in the efficient comprehension of narrative texts.
nodes.rag_problem: During narrative text comprehension, readers need to construct knowledge-based inferences to fully understand and integrate information, but it is often unclear which inferences are necessary and how they are generated.

nodes.title: Entertainment?Education and Elaboration Likelihood: Understanding the Processing of Narrative Persuasion

deep_analysis.title: Entertainment?Education and Elaboration Likelihood: Understanding the Processing of Narrative Persuasion
deep_analysis.problem: Entertainment-education messages impact beliefs, attitudes, and behavior, but there is limited understanding of how narrative processing enhances persuasion beyond social cognitive theory.
deep_analysis.method:
Utilizing elaboration likelihood approaches focused on absorption in narrative and character responses to enhance persuasive effects.

**Explanation:** Elaboration likelihood focuses on the cognitive processing depth when individuals are absorbed in a narrative. This absorption increases engagement with the message and identification with characters, enhancing persuasion because individuals are more likely to process the content meaningfully and relate it to their own beliefs and behaviors.

- Research could examine the role of specific character attributes or audience identification with characters in enhancing the persuasive effects of narratives.
- Further studies could investigate the interaction between the level of audience involvement in a narrative and the effectiveness of the persuasive message it conveys.

nodes.title: Causal thinking and the representation of narrative events

deep_analysis.title: Causal thinking and the representation of narrative events
deep_analysis.problem: Difficulty in understanding how causal relationships are represented within narrative events.
deep_analysis.method:
Develop a framework or model that identifies and categorizes causal connections between events in narratives.

**Explanation:** By establishing a systematic approach to categorize and identify causal relationships in narratives, readers can better understand the sequence and impact of events. This framework enhances comprehension by providing a structured way to perceive how certain events cause other events within narratives, thus facilitating deeper understanding and analysis.

nodes.title: Inference during reading.

deep_analysis.title: Inference during reading.
deep_analysis.problem: The complexity and assumptions of current theories in text processing which rely heavily on constructionist views, often making it challenging to discern what inferences are automatically encoded during reading.
deep_analysis.method:
The minimalist hypothesis, which posits that automatic inferences during reading only occur based on easily available information from explicit text statements or general knowledge, and are necessary for local coherence.

**Explanation:** The minimalist hypothesis simplifies inference processing by reducing the number of assumptions required. It suggests that only essential inferences supporting local coherence and those easily derived from explicit or general information are encoded during reading, thus addressing the complexity issue posed by constructionist theories.

nodes.title: Chatbots: Are they Really Useful?

deep_analysis.title: Chatbots: Are they Really Useful?
deep_analysis.problem: Chatbots have historically struggled to provide meaningful interactions in diverse domains beyond mere entertainment due to limited understanding and poor pattern matching techniques.
deep_analysis.method:
Utilization of the ALICE/AIML architecture to enable chatbots to be retrained using dialogue corpora to achieve more domain-specific understanding, and the development of automated tools to convert texts into AIML format.

**Explanation:** The ALICE/AIML framework supports pattern matching by using predefined categories, allowing a chatbot to simulate human dialogue. By retraining ALICE using dialogue corpora specific to different domains, chatbots can better match user intent and provide relevant information beyond entertainment. The automated conversion tools streamline the process of adapting the chatbot to new topics or languages by efficiently generating AIML files from corpora. This method enhances the chatbot's ability to deliver domain-specific knowledge and assist in various applications such as education and information retrieval.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The method struggles with fully replacing the human role, as it is aimed at helping rather than completely substituting human interaction or imitating human conversation perfectly.
- The system may not understand complex or ambiguous queries, which could necessitate redirection to other channels such as e-mail or live chat when faced with comprehension issues.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Develop Chatterbots for specific educational purposes such as talking books for children and foreign language instruction to enhance learning experiences without replacing human teachers entirely.
- Enhance e-commerce systems like the Happy Assistant by improving the explanation of recommendations to build trust and ensure users understand the rationale behind choices made by the system.
- Explore the use of fictional characters in virtual environments, as demonstrated by Sanelma in museums, to provide engaging educational content and context-specific interactions for visitors.
- Implement versatile assistant chatbots like Rita in various domains where they can efficiently redirect the user to alternative communication channels if misunderstandings occur, ensuring effective problem resolution.

problem_evidence.text: The paper discusses retraining ALICE with corpora and pattern matching enhancements for educational purposes and information retrieval, highlighting improvements in user satisfaction and task-oriented interactions.

method_evidence.text: The paper discusses retraining ALICE with corpora and pattern matching enhancements for educational purposes and information retrieval, highlighting improvements in user satisfaction and task-oriented interactions.

limitation_evidence.text: Student: Hello. Patient: Hello, doctor. Student: What brought you into the hospital today? Patient: I have a crushing feeling in my chest, and I'm worried it might be something serious. Student: Does it hurt? Patient: I have a heaviness in my chest. Student: Is it spreading? Patient: My chest pain has spread to my left shoulder, neck, and jaw. Student: What did you eat last night? Patient: I had pizza for supper, which I can usually eat plenty of without getting sick. Student: How often do you drink? Patient: I drink beer on weekends but usually not much else. Student: Do you have any problems with your heart? Patient: I do not have any heart problems that I know of. Student: When did the pain start? Patient: I have been having the discomfort for about 2 hours. When it didn't go away with Tylenol and Motrin, I decided to come in. Student: Are you allergic to any medications? Patient: I am not allergic to anything that I know of. 7 Chatbot Assistants in E-Commerce, Business, and other Domains

Shopping assistant is one of the most persuasive sales in traditional commerce. Shopping assistant offer help in a store, provide additional information on products and simplify decision making process helping to find a good that satisfies customer's requirements and various constraints. (Bogdanovych et al., 2005) Happy Assistant is "a natural language dialog-based navigation system that helps users access e-commerce sites to find relevant information about products and services" (Chai et al., 2000) . The system is composed of three main modules: the presenta-Band 22 (1) -2007 tion manager (PM), the dialog Manager (DM), and the Action Manager (AC). The presentation manager applies a shallow parsing technique to identify semantic and syntactic information of interest from the user textual input. Then it translates the user's input into a well formed XML message called the logical form. The dialogue manager is responsible for matching concepts from user's query to business rules found in the knowledge domain. The business rules consist of a list of concepts together with some meta data about the target product or service. If a match is found, the webpage associated with that rule is presented to the user. Otherwise, the most important missing concept is figured out by introducing questions to the user. The role now is turned to the action manager, which accesses the product that matched the query, and if the user provides special preferences, a sorting algorithm is applied to yield a ranked list of products. To make users trust the system, system must offer some explanation before producing a result, so the system summarizes user's request by paraphrasing it using the context history. Figure 7 presents a sample of conversation with the Happy Assistant System taken from Chai and Lin (2001) .

U: I am looking for a notebook for my consulting business S: Please describe your financial constraints. In this stage a list of most popular ones is displayed U: not important thing performance is essential S: are you looking for something that is top of the line? U: Yes, absolutely S: I have found something for you. The target notebook is displayed for the user. And beneath it a summary of the users request displayed to explain why this product is displayed.

limitation_evidence.section: Figure 7: A sample of chatting with the Happy Assistant system
limitation_evidence.text: Evaluations and results showed that users found it easy to use, meeting the users' needs, users like the idea that they could express their needs in their language, users feel that the computer did all the work for them, and moreover users found that the system reduced the interaction time. Sanelma (2003) is a fictional person to talk with in a museum, which provides background information concerning a certain piece of art. Sanelma is a 26 year old woman from Helsinki of the 30's as shown in figure 8 . Rita (real time Internet technical assistant), an eGain graphical avatar, is used in the ABN AMRO Bank to help customer doing some financial tasks such as a wire money transfer (Voth, 2005) . If Rita does not understand, it can redirect the customer to another channel such as an e-mail or live chat. 8 Conclusion

limitation_evidence.section: Figure 7: A sample of chatting with the Happy Assistant system
limitation_evidence.text: We have surveyed several chatbot systems which succeed in practical domains like education, information retrieval, business, e-commerce, as well as for amusement. In the future, you could "imagine Chatterbots acting as talking books for children, Chatterbots for foreign language instruction, and teaching Chatterbots in general." (Wallace et al., 2003) . However, in the education domain Knill et al. (2004) concluded that "the teacher is the backbone in the teaching process. Technology like computer algebra systems, multimedia presentations or 'chatbots' can serve as amplifiers but not replace a good guide". In general, the aim of chatbot designers should be: to build tools that help people, facilitate their work, and their interaction with computers using natural language; but not to replace the human role totally, or imitate human conversation perfectly. Finally, as Colby (1999) states, "We need not take human-human conversation as the gold standard for conversational exchanges. If one had a perfect simulation of a human conversant, then it would be human-human conversation and not humancomputer conversation with its sometimes odd but pertinent properties."

nodes.title: Question Answering in Restricted Domains: An Overview

deep_analysis.title: Question Answering in Restricted Domains: An Overview
deep_analysis.problem: Automated question answering systems require efficient handling of domain-specific knowledge to provide accurate responses, yet many systems struggle to do so in restricted domains due to limitations in knowledge representation and retrieval.
deep_analysis.method:
Adopt a methodology that leverages text collections as primary knowledge sources, supplemented by domain-specific enhancements, to effectively manage and retrieve domain-specific knowledge for question answering.

**Explanation:** By utilizing text collections as the main knowledge base, systems can access a broader range of information relevant to specific domains. This approach allows for the integration of domain-specific data, which minimizes the limitations of traditional hand-encoded knowledge bases. The methodology focuses on adapting text parsing and retrieval techniques to better understand and provide accurate answers within restricted domains, thus addressing the issue of efficient knowledge representation and retrieval.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- Our method is focused on restricted domains, which limits its applicability to a broader range of open-domain questions, thereby restricting the generalization capabilities of the approach.
- The reliance on domain-specific text collections as the main knowledge source means that our approach may not effectively handle questions that require information outside of these restricted datasets.
- Despite improvements in computing power and methodology, our method still faces challenges with complex domains that require deep contextual understanding and integration from diverse sources.
deep_analysis.future_work: 未找到明确的未来工作描述

Fatih Taşar, Associate Editor Gazi Üniversitesi, Ankara, TURKEY Dear members of EURASIA family, On behalf of the editors of EURASIA I would like to express my deepest gratitute to our readers, authors, and reviewers. Thanks to their great efforts we are able to publish our fourth volume. In this issue there are 8 papers and a conversation/interview between Sandra Abell and Patricia Friedrichsen of University of Missouri-Columbia on science teacher learning. I strongly believe that when in time this genre of writing develops in EURASIA (and perhaps also elsewhere) together with the audio recordings they will prove to be a great source of information for future researchers. It can also be forseen that these documents can become a good resource for graduate education in our field.

I am also happy to inform you that as an Endeavour Executive Awardee I will be travelling to Australia to spend four months at RMIT University. Professor Annette Gough has kind ly offered to be my host during my scholarly work there. I am sure it will be a most benefial time period for my work. I will engage in activities for understanding the school system, teacher education, teacher development and curricula in the state of Victoria.

nodes.title: The um toolkit for cooperative user modelling

deep_analysis.title: The um toolkit for cooperative user modelling
deep_analysis.problem: Difficulty in developing cooperative user models that effectively adapt applications to diverse user needs.
deep_analysis.method:
The UM toolkit provides a framework for creating cooperative user models that facilitate efficient adaptation based on user input and profile data.

**Explanation:** The UM toolkit allows developers to create models that continuously update and refine user profiles through cooperative interaction, ensuring that applications can dynamically adapt to the user's changing preferences and contexts. By leveraging cooperative mechanisms, the toolkit ensures user models remain relevant and personalized, thus solving the issue of static or non-responsive user adaptation in software applications.

nodes.title: A tutoring and student modelling paradigm for gaming environments

deep_analysis.title: A tutoring and student modelling paradigm for gaming environments
deep_analysis.problem: In gaming environments, there is a need for personalized feedback and guidance to help students improve without human intervention.
deep_analysis.method:
The system constructs a 'differential model' by comparing the student's moves to those of an 'Expert' player, thereby identifying areas where the student's behavior deviates from ideal strategies.

**Explanation:** By using an 'Expert' player as a benchmark, the system can automatically analyze the student's gameplay and provide specific, targeted feedback on the differences. This allows the system to offer personalized hints and guidance, helping the student learn and adjust their strategies more effectively.

nodes.title: The language of genes

deep_analysis.title: The language of genes
deep_analysis.problem: The estimated immense degrees of freedom for living systems appear to contradict the holographic principle, which imposes a strong constraint on the maximum information content of the observed universe.
deep_analysis.method:
The authors propose reconciling this contradiction through the concept of the 'language of genes,' which acts as an additional constraint on the degrees of freedom by restricting the permissible sequences of macromolecules to those with causality to initial states, effectively reducing the possible states of living systems.

**Explanation:** The introduction of a causality constraint reduces the number of permissible states when considering the 'language of genes' in protein sequences. Instead of all possible protein sequences being viable, only those that maintain causal relationships with initial states (as defined by the holographic principle) are valid. This effectively reduces the estimated degrees of freedom to a number that aligns with the entropy bound of the universe.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- Our model relies heavily on the tree adjoining grammar and the assumption of a single variant, which may oversimplify the complex mechanisms involved in protein sequence generation.
- The cutoff of protein length at c = 3000 may limit the method's ability to accurately represent longer sequences, potentially omitting important biological information.
- The reliance on discrete Fourier transformation to analyze protein length distributions might not capture all nuances of the correlation between protein length and species, as periodic-like fluctuations may have multiple contributing factors.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Explore the underlying mechanisms in protein sequence generation further by expanding the proposed model based on tree adjoining grammar and investigating new variants that influence substitution probabilities.
- Investigate the intrinsic correlation among protein length distributions across different species by analyzing the frequency distribution patterns observed in the lf m plane, focusing on periodic-like fluctuations.
- Develop advanced simulation techniques to enhance the model of protein length distribution fluctuations, with an emphasis on integrating additional grammar rules to capture species-specific distribution characteristics.

limitation_evidence.section: THE LANGUAGE OF GENES AND UNDERLYING ORDER IN SEQUENCES
limitation_evidence.text: Let f m denotes the frequency of the highest peak D(f m ) in the discrete fourier transformation of the protein length distribution for a species. We found that there is an interesting relationship between the frequency f m and the average protein length l of species. The distribution of species in lf m plane shows an regular pattern: the species in the three domains (Archaebacteria, Eubacteria and Eukaryotes) gathered in three rainbow-like arches respectively (Fig 3 ). This pattern strongly indicates the intrinsic correlation among the protein length distributions, which can never achieve if the protein length distributions are stochastic. The periodic-like fluctuations in the protein length distribution [21] may also originate in the underlying mechanism of generation of protein sequences.

limitation_evidence.section: EXPLANATION OF THE ORDER IN PROTEIN SEQUENCES
limitation_evidence.text: We propose a model to reveal the underlying mechanism in the protein sequences according to tree adjoining grammar [22] . In the model, protein sequences can be generated by tree adjoining operations, i.e., substituting the initial tree or auxiliary trees into to each other by identifying the inner nodes (Fig. 4a ) [22] . There is only one variant t in the model, which is the probability of substitutions in the adjoining operations and denotes different species. A certain number of proteins can be generated when t is fixed, hence we obtain a protein length distribution by the model (Fig. 4b ). The properties of protein length distributions can be explained by the simulation. The outline and the fluctuations of the simulated protein length distribution agree with the actual protein length distributions in principle.

limitation_evidence.section: EXPLANATION OF THE ORDER IN PROTEIN SEQUENCES
limitation_evidence.text: We show that there is a close relationship between the protein length distributions and grammar rules. The fluctuations in the distributions are determined by the grammar rules. The same grammar rule corresponds to the same distribution. If changing grammar rules, we obtain different outlines and fluctuations of distribution. This result suggests that the fluctuations in actual protein length distributions are intrinsic properties of certain species and may infer the underlying mechanism on the order of protein sequences.

nodes.title: Corpus Research Applications in Second Language Teaching

deep_analysis.title: Corpus Research Applications in Second Language Teaching
deep_analysis.problem: Corpora and corpus tools have not been widely implemented in language teaching despite their potential benefits.
deep_analysis.method:
The article provides an overview of pedagogical corpus applications to bridge the gap between corpus linguistics and language pedagogy.

**Explanation:** By highlighting specific applications of corpus research in language teaching, the article aims to demonstrate the practical benefits and uses of corpus tools. This can help educators understand and adopt these resources in their teaching practices, effectively integrating empirical linguistic data to enhance language education.

nodes.title: Multilingual children increase language differentiation by indexing communities of practice

deep_analysis.title: Multilingual children increase language differentiation by indexing communities of practice
deep_analysis.problem: Multilingual children face challenges in language differentiation due to complex multilingual contexts where languages are not clearly separated by interlocutor or domain.
deep_analysis.method:
The study uses a community of practice model to understand how multilingual children index language differentiation through social practices.

**Explanation:** By applying the community of practice model, the study explores how multilingual children can differentiate their use of language by associating it with specific social groups and practices. This model emphasizes the role of language as a social practice and suggests that children construct their identities and language differentiation through their participation in different communities. Thus, by indexing communities of practice, children can increase language differentiation and navigate multilingual contexts more effectively.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The method may not fully account for the complexity of multilingual contexts where language separation by interlocutor or domain is minimal, as it focuses on language differentiation through community practices.
- There is limited understanding of how multilingual children use languages for identity construction, which could affect the applicability of the method in diverse linguistic settings.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Investigate how multilingual children develop and use language differentiation strategies in diverse social contexts to construct their identities uniquely in each language or across languages.
- Expand research on identity construction in multilingual settings by adapting the community of practice model, which has been primarily applied and examined in monolingual environments or less complex multilingual situations.

nodes.title: Applying corpus methods to written academic texts: Explorations of MICUSP

deep_analysis.title: Applying corpus methods to written academic texts: Explorations of MICUSP
deep_analysis.problem: Lack of a basic introduction to corpus linguistic methods tailored for writing researchers, specifically to investigate academic writing.
deep_analysis.method:
The paper provides an introduction to central corpus analysis techniques using the Michigan Corpus of Upper-level Student Papers (MICUSP) and demonstrates its application via AntConc software.

**Explanation:** By introducing core techniques like word lists, keyword lists, concordances, and clusters, and demonstrating the use of AntConc for corpus analysis, the paper equips writing researchers with the knowledge to explore writing data in a structured, efficient manner. This structured approach enables understanding and discovering linguistic features that might be unnoticed without corpus methods.
deep_analysis.limitation:
- The method may struggle with generalizing findings across different corpora or linguistic contexts beyond the specific MICUSP data, potentially limiting the broader applicability of the results.
- While frequency lists and type-token ratios provide valuable insights, they may not capture the full complexity of linguistic patterns and variations that exist in different disciplines, possibly limiting the depth of analysis.
- The reliance on chi-square tests to identify differences between disciplines may not adequately address more nuanced or subtle distinctions, potentially overlooking smaller yet significant patterns.
deep_analysis.future_work:
- Future work could explore additional linguistic phenomena beyond attended and unattended "this" within different academic genres by applying corpus methods to provide deeper insights into these variations.
- Further research should be conducted to investigate interdisciplinary differences in language use across varied academic divisions or faculties to avoid the blurring effects seen in previous studies.
- There is potential to examine the frequency and distribution of other cohesive devices across disciplines, leveraging corpus analysis to reveal patterns that contribute to effective academic writing practices.
- Plans could include expanding the range of normalized frequency analyses to uncover more subtle discipline-specific trends in academic writing, thus enhancing the understanding of language use in student papers.

problem_evidence.text: The present paper seeks to take a first step toward closing this gap [...] we will illustrate, by means of a case study, how corpus tools can be employed to highlight important aspects of a text or text collection that may go unnoticed otherwise.

method_evidence.text: The present paper seeks to take a first step toward closing this gap [...] we will illustrate, by means of a case study, how corpus tools can be employed to highlight important aspects of a text or text collection that may go unnoticed otherwise.

 by calculating the type-token ratios for the head nouns accompanying this in the different disciplines, we found that disciplines vary considerably with regard to the diversity of head nouns employed;

 a frequency list of the most common head nouns, combined with a list of the nouns most widely dispersed across the disciplines, supported earlier work on academic writing, emphasizing the overall similarities between the disciplines in their use of attended this as a cohesive device that links preceding and subsequent argumentation by providing metadiscoursal links and initiating methodology-related explanation.

future_work_evidence.text: By way of exploring a pre-release version of the Michigan Corpus of Upper-level Student Papers (MICUSP_Jan09), this article has discussed how corpus linguistics and corpus methods can contribute to writing research, in particular research on advanced student academic writing. We have provided a basic introduction to what we consider core techniques in corpus analysis. A central aim was to show how software tools for corpus access enable users to see things that would be hard (or impossible, even) to see if the texts in a corpus were accessed without the help of such tools. One major advantage of a corpus/software-based approach to texts over a manual (non-computerbased) approach is that a much larger amount of language data can be examined in a short period of time, and new aspects about language (in our case student academic writing) can be captured and described. As Sinclair (1991) rightfully states, ''[t]he language looks different when you look at a lot of it at once'' (p. 100). The usefulness of corpus analysis was then further exemplified through a case study of attended and unattended this in MICUSP_Jan09. 4 In this case study we saw that the creation and sorting of a concordance and the retrieval of information on the textual distribution of a word are powerful analytic techniques that may highlight usage patterns across disciplinary subsets of our corpus. The case study also demonstrated that it is important to treat disciplines separately in comparative analyses since groupings (e.g. according to academic divisions or faculties) may blur inter-disciplinary differences. While our case study was limited to one specific phenomenon ([un] attended this) in one specific genre (academic writing) by a specifically defined population (students), we hope to have given readers a taste of the possibilities offered by corpus linguistic methodology. Corpus methods are powerful in that they reveal patterns in the data that would otherwise escape the naked eye. While corpora and corpus tools will not do all the work for the writing researcher, they will help her/him discover phenomena that are worth investigating and highlight the preferred usage patterns of these phenomena.
